reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P sync (downloadByRoot/downloadByRange) by sending a DataColumnSidecar where column.length != kzgCommitments.length, causing mismatched array lengths (cellIndices/cells use column.length while commitments/proofs use kzgCommitments.length) to be passed to the native KZG library asyncVerifyCellKzgProofBatch, potentially causing incorrect verification, crash, or undefined behavior. The code validates kzgProofs.length == kzgCommitments.length (L324) but fails to validate column.length == kzgCommitments.length before building arrays at L345-348. Spec reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, invariant PROP-6a4369e9-inv-037 requires len(commitments) == len(cell_indices) == len(cells) == len(proofs). Severity calibrated to High per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json: single-client bug affecting >33% network share (Lodestar in minority position), P2P-exploitable with single malicious packet.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch must receive consistent-length arrays for valid verification. Assertion: len(commitments) == len(cell_indices) == len(cells) == len(proofs)
